title: Cross-fitted partial-pooling encoders for Driver/Race history
summary: Current best code uses frequency counts only, but the data has very high-cardinality `Driver` and race-context categories.
rationale: Current best code uses frequency counts only, but the data has very high-cardinality `Driver` and race-context categories. Regularized target encoding and GLMM-style shrinkage usually beat raw frequency features because they borrow strength across rare levels while reducing overfit.
implementation_hint: Replace or augment `_freq` features with fold-safe encodings for `Driver`, `Race`, `Race_Compound`, `Driver_Compound`, `Race_Year`, using chronological or grouped OOF fits only. Test GLMM/log-odds shrinkage and regularized target encoding, with priors estimated from earlier folds only.
expected_effect: Better use of historical pit tendency by driver/race/compound, likely improving AUC on sparse categories without relying on leakage-prone full-data means.
risk: If encodings are not strictly OOF and time-safe, local CV will be inflated. Rare 2025-only categories may still need fallback-to-global priors.
